rows_detail.boundary: The cover letter still needs author, editor, reviewer, and prior-dissemination fields before upload.
rows_detail.item: Cover letter preserves logical-layer scope
rows_detail.supported_use: The cover letter can introduce the manuscript without overclaiming hardware mapping or physical execution.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Cover-letter prose could accidentally frame the paper as a hardware compiler.

rows_detail.boundary: It should not be read as universal dominance over every synthesis or compilation method.
rows_detail.item: Cover letter exposes the comparison envelope
rows_detail.supported_use: The cover letter summarizes layered baselines and directs editors to the comparison evidence.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Editor may see only a narrow SSHR comparison or miss external-toolchain evidence.

rows_detail.boundary: The scorecard indexes existing evidence; it does not add a new metric or broaden the logical-layer claim.
rows_detail.item: Novelty/comparison scorecard is visible
rows_detail.supported_use: The support packet gives editors and reviewers a compact route from comparison questions to evidence and limitations.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Reviewer-facing comparison meaning could be scattered across too many tables and briefs.

rows_detail.boundary: The validity audit classifies existing comparisons; it does not convert secondary probes into full hardware-mapped baselines.
rows_detail.item: Comparison target validity audit is visible
rows_detail.supported_use: The support packet gives reviewers a direct route from comparison targets to role labels and excluded claims.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Reviewer may not know which comparisons are primary benchmarks, external probes, controls, or counterpoints.

rows_detail.boundary: The route audit indexes existing comparison evidence; it does not add a new result or broaden the logical-layer claim.
rows_detail.item: Comparison route decision audit is visible
rows_detail.supported_use: The support packet maps each reviewer-facing claim to the comparator family that can answer it and the over-claim that remains excluded.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Reviewer may know the baseline names but still read all comparator families as one universal leaderboard.

rows_detail.boundary: The benchmark-suite audit describes existing evidence; it does not expand coverage to all Boolean functions or hardware-mapped benchmarks.
rows_detail.item: Benchmark-suite audit is visible from reviewer entrypoints
rows_detail.supported_use: Reviewer-facing entrypoints expose which benchmark suites, n scopes, verification routes, and representativeness boundaries support the comparisons.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Reviewers could see comparison outcomes without the benchmark-slice composition and representativeness boundary.

rows_detail.boundary: It is a navigation and wording guide over existing evidence; it does not add experiments or replace author/venue metadata.
rows_detail.item: Chinese comparison handoff is visible
rows_detail.supported_use: The author-facing Chinese handoff keeps comparison, novelty, and excluded-claim language aligned before upload or reviewer response.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Author-side upload text or reviewer replies could overstate SSHR, ROS, RevKit, or AI/MCTS claims.

rows_detail.boundary: The matrix summarizes existing verified comparison evidence; it does not introduce a new result or broaden the comparison scope.
rows_detail.item: Chinese comparison significance matrix is visible
rows_detail.supported_use: The author can map each baseline family to its role, supported conclusion, invalid conclusion, and evidence entry point before drafting upload text or rebuttals.
rows_detail.upload_risk: The author may know the baseline names but not which conclusion each comparison can legitimately support.

rows_detail.boundary: This checks evidence-entrypoint integrity; it does not add new experiments or strengthen any comparison claim beyond the referenced artifacts.
rows_detail.item: Comparison evidence entrypoints resolve
rows_detail.supported_use: The comparison handoff now has a machine-checked path from each cited evidence file or table name to an existing artifact.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Author-facing comparison handoffs could cite stale evidence filenames that cannot be opened during upload preparation or reviewer response drafting.

rows_detail.boundary: Final authorship, funding, conflicts, archive links, and AI disclosure remain human-gated.
rows_detail.item: Author declarations keep private fields explicit
rows_detail.supported_use: The declarations packet is a structured intake path with a short answer file rather than a hidden or implicit author task.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Declarations could be mistaken as complete or private author metadata could be committed.

rows_detail.boundary: The questionnaire is an intake guide only; it does not contain, infer, or commit private author values.
rows_detail.item: Chinese metadata questionnaire maps human inputs
rows_detail.supported_use: The author can answer a Chinese field-by-field questionnaire and transfer the answers to the ignored private metadata JSON.
rows_detail.upload_risk: The final author/venue gate could remain too abstract for the author to fill in one pass.

rows_detail.boundary: The short form still contains prompts only; real author and venue values remain private and human-provided.
rows_detail.item: Minimal response form covers required metadata fields
rows_detail.supported_use: The concise Chinese response form is machine-checked against every required metadata path before authors transfer answers to ignored private JSON.
rows_detail.upload_risk: The author may use only the short response form and accidentally omit a required private field.

rows_detail.boundary: This only audits public prompts; private author and venue values remain outside the repository.
rows_detail.item: Minimal response-form coverage is visible from reviewer entrypoints
rows_detail.supported_use: The public guide, README, and checklist now point to the machine check proving the concise author-intake form covers every required metadata path.
rows_detail.upload_risk: The short author form could be present but its machine-checked required-field coverage could be hard to find.

rows_detail.boundary: The template contains placeholders only; real author and venue values remain private and human-provided.
rows_detail.item: Short answer template covers metadata fields
rows_detail.supported_use: The tracked short-answer JSON template is machine-checked against every required private metadata path before authors fill the ignored answer file.
rows_detail.upload_risk: The public JSON answer template could omit a required private field even if the longer questionnaire mentions it.

rows_detail.boundary: The audit does not choose the journal; it only checks that the choice is explicit and policy-gated.
rows_detail.item: Venue policy gate is visible before upload
rows_detail.supported_use: The venue-choice step is concrete enough for the author to fill the private metadata file.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Target venue style, anonymous review, AI disclosure, or archive policy could be missed.

rows_detail.boundary: It records public policy entry points and author actions; it does not fill private metadata or guarantee a venue decision.
rows_detail.item: Target-venue policy checklist is actionable
rows_detail.supported_use: The policy checklist maps public ACM TQC, Quantum, and archive/license gates to the private metadata fields that must be filled before upload.
rows_detail.upload_risk: The author could choose a venue but miss the specific metadata fields implied by the venue policy.

rows_detail.boundary: Tracked audit outputs remain public and redacted; private generated Markdown files stay ignored.
rows_detail.item: Private preview path remains safe
rows_detail.supported_use: Authors can generate private submission-system text after filling metadata without committing it.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Generated cover-letter/declaration previews could leak private metadata into Git or the payload.

rows_detail.boundary: Anonymous-review policy and anonymous archive links still require target-venue input.
rows_detail.item: Anonymous-review fork is not conflated with author version
rows_detail.supported_use: The support packet separates the current author-labeled manuscript from the venue-dependent anonymous path.
rows_detail.upload_risk: A double-blind venue could receive author-labeled material or non-anonymous links.

rows_detail.boundary: Triage briefs are navigation aids, not acceptance guarantees.
rows_detail.item: Editor and reviewer triage is aligned
rows_detail.supported_use: The editor-facing and reviewer-facing triage path points to the same bounded evidence package.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Cover letter, editor brief, and reviewer brief could emphasize different claims or boundaries.

rows_detail.boundary: The checklist cannot replace final venue-system proof review.
rows_detail.item: Upload checklist cites terminal verifiers
rows_detail.supported_use: The public handoff points authors to the same terminal checks used by the repository.
rows_detail.upload_risk: Final upload could skip the one-command package verifier or the support-packet audit.
